assertions.description: document.requestStorageAccess remains available — RWS removal does not affect the Storage Access API itself.

Fetching /.well-known/related-website-set.json from another origin does not produce side-effects in browser policy (this is hard to feature-detect; we probe by checking the page can construct a request to that URL without error).
cookieStore (if present) does not expose a related-website-set membership accessor (no such IDL existed; this guards against an accidental re-add).
assertions.test: (()=>{if(!('cookieStore' in self))return true;return !('relatedWebsiteSets' in cookieStore) && !('relatedWebsiteSet' in cookieStore);})()

assertions.description: navigator.permissions.query({name:'storage-access'}) still resolves; the user-grant path is preserved post-RWS removal.
Setting a SameSite=Lax cookie via document.cookie is still possible — SameSite enforcement (which RWS partly relaxed) remains live.
